summary: Decompiled and typed a substantial part of player trick/stat tracking code in src/melee/pl/pltrick.c, replacing several placeholder stubs with C implementations for attack/action accounting, hit tracking, damage event bookkeeping, and special kind validation. The PR also refined related fighter/player structs and function prototypes so pltrick could call stale-move, bonus, magnify, ftdata, and damage-event helpers with more accurate argument types.
what_changed: Implemented pl_80037C60, which updates player action statistics from Fighter.x2070 state, including attack totals, per-attack counts, thrown item/aerial/special counters, and several x5BC flags.

Added a static helper fn_80037F00 in pltrick.c to populate Fighter.dmg.x18d8 damage/event data from an ft_800898B4_t event or reset it to defaults, then derive extra flags from magnify state, motion_id 0xFD, prior knockback, source player, and ft_80089914 checks.

Implemented pl_80038144, handling attacker/victim hit bookkeeping: behind-hit detection, stale/bonus library calls, hit count updates, best-hit tracking, team-related stale move logic, and storage of attack instance data on the victim fighter.
Implemented pl_800384DC, a local/self hit-stat update path that mirrors much of the hit and best-hit accounting used by pl_80038144, then sets Fighter.x2074.x2084_b7.
Implemented pl_80038628, using a new pl_803BCE70 lookup table and ftData_80085FD4 to validate whether a kind in the 1..0x10 range is available/allowed.
Added pltrick.c dependencies on ft_0892.h, ftdata.h, ifmagnify.h, plbonus.h, plbonuslib.h, and pl_040D.h to support the new decompiled logic.
Refined struct Fighter::Struct2074 offset 0x2084 from a raw u32 to a union containing the raw word and named one-bit u8 flags x2084_b0 through x2084_b7.
Changed ft_800898B4_t bitfields at offsets +0x10 and +0x11 from u32 bitfields to u8 bitfields, matching byte-addressed flag usage in the new pltrick code.
Adjusted pl_804D6470_t so offset +0x14 is represented as float x14 rather than opaque padding; fn_80037F00 compares previous knockback against this field.

Corrected several player bonus/stale function signatures to include additional int arguments, apparently representing subplayer/costume-like x221F_b4 or related parameters: pl_8003ED0C, pl_8003FE40, pl_8003FE64, pl_80040D8C, pl_80040FBC, and pl_800410F4.

Updated pltrick.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or imprecise signatures to concrete return and argument types for pl_80037C60, pl_80038144, and pl_800384DC; fn_80037F00 became static in pltrick.c and was removed from the public header.
smart_moves: Used a union for Struct2074.x2084 so future code can access either the full raw word or individual bit flags without losing offset/layout evidence.
Converted ft_800898B4_t flags to u8 bitfields, aligning declarations with offsets +0x10 and +0x11 and making byte-granular flag assignments in fn_80037F00 natural.
Kept fn_80037F00 static because the diff only shows internal use from pltrick.c, reducing header exposure and avoiding exporting a helper with uncertain external semantics.
Introduced a tiny inline wrapper pl_80037BC0_inline around pl_80037BC0, likely to influence inlining/codegen at call sites while preserving the existing public helper.

Carried attacker_fp->x221F_b4 and victim_fp->x221F_b4 through many plbonuslib/pl_040D calls instead of treating those parameters as unused, which clarified several function signatures and call contracts.
Represented the attack/kind mapping as a fixed 16-entry table pl_803BCE70 with -1 sentinels, making pl_80038628’s kind bounds and data lookup explicit.
decomp_lessons: When a function’s callers pass player_id plus x221F_b4 pairs, do not leave the second argument as UNK_T or omit it from prototypes; propagate the concrete int parameter through headers and call sites to expose the real calling convention.
For packed fighter flags, preserving both raw-word access and per-bit names through a union is useful when different matched functions need different access patterns.
Byte-offset comments on bitfields matter: u32 bitfields can be misleading when decompiled code clearly manipulates flag bytes at adjacent offsets.
Matching stat-accounting code often requires reloading packed event unions into multiple local variables; separate union temporaries can help reproduce original register/stack behavior.
A small inline wrapper around an already-existing helper may be a matching tactic when direct calls do not reproduce expected inlining or stack/register layout.

The PR contains explicit PAD_STACK uses and a volatile previous-event word in pl_80037C60, showing that stack/register shape was still a sensitive matching concern even after high-level behavior was recovered.
Public headers should be tightened as decompilation proceeds: replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete prototypes, and demote helpers to static when evidence shows file-local use.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as pl_80037C60, pl_80038144, pl_800384DC, pl_80038628, and fn_80037F00.
Struct and field names remain offset-based when semantics are incomplete, for example x2074_vec, x207C, x2084_b7, x18d8, x18d4, x221F_b4, and pl_804D6470_t.x14.
Boolean bitfields are named with byte/bit suffixes such as x10_b0, x10_b7, x11_b4, and x2084_b7.
Player/action stat locals use short descriptive abbreviations where known, such as acp for plActionStats, fp for Fighter, attacker_fp, victim_fp, attack_id, and attacked_from_behind.
Unknown or partially understood event data remains named ev, ev_data, ev_reload, ev_best, and ev_hits, reflecting its packed Struct2070/ft_800898B4_t role without overclaiming semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used volatile s32 prev2070_word in pl_80037C60 with a comment noting the stack was still misaligned for the volatile hack, suggesting this was a deliberate codegen attempt rather than a semantic need.
Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in fn_80037F00 and PAD_STACK(16) in pl_80038144/pl_800384DC to match stack frame shape.
Removed an obsolete PAD_STACK(8) from pl_8003ED0C after correcting its signature to include an extra int argument, implying the prior stack padding was compensating for an incorrect prototype.
Used repeated union Struct2070 copies/reloads before calling pl_80037BC0_inline, likely to reproduce original temporary/register behavior around packed attack event fields.

Kept explicit casts such as *(s32*) &victim_fp->dmg.x18d4 and (HSD_GObj**) attacker_fp in the diff; the latter looks suspicious from semantics but is evidence of a matching-oriented expression in this slice.
Modeled Struct2074.x2084 as a union of u32 and u8 bitfields to allow both word-level and bit-level codegen access.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No human PR body text was included, so intent beyond the title and diff must be inferred from code changes.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that revisit pl_80037C60 stack alignment or remove the volatile prev2070_word hack.
Search for follow-up fixes around fn_80037F00, especially the gp argument and the cast used at the pl_80038144 call site.
Check subsequent work on ft_800898B4_t and Struct2074 to see whether x10/x11/x2084 bitfields receive semantic names.
Find later PRs that rename or fully document pl_80038144, pl_800384DC, and pl_80038628.
Search for additional corrections to plbonuslib/pl_040D signatures involving player_id and x221F_b4 pairs.
